context: Tests whether the agent implements all four standard attribution models correctly, including the time-decay half-life, channel key format, fractional order counting, empty path handling, and multi-model comparison output.

checklist.name: All four models present
checklist.description: The code implements all four attribution functions: last-click, first-click, linear, and time-decay (not just one or two)

checklist.name: Time-decay half-life = 7 days
checklist.description: The time-decay model uses a half-life of exactly 7 days — i.e., the exponent divides days-before-conversion by 7 (not 14, 30, or another value)

checklist.name: Time-decay uses exponential decay
checklist.description: Time-decay weights are computed using Math.pow(0.5, daysBeforeConversion / halfLifeDays) or equivalent — not a linear ramp

checklist.name: Channel key format: source/medium
checklist.description: Channel keys in the aggregation output are formatted as 'source/medium' (e.g., 'google/cpc'), not just 'source' or some other format

checklist.name: Fractional order counting
checklist.description: Order count attribution uses fractional values (credit / orderRevenue) rather than incrementing by 1 per order

checklist.name: Empty path fallback: direct/none
checklist.description: When a conversion path has zero touchpoints, the attribution assigns credit to source='direct', medium='none', campaign='(none)'

checklist.name: Multi-model comparison produced
checklist.description: The results.json or output includes channel revenue figures under multiple attribution models side-by-side (not just one model)

checklist.name: Discrepancy analysis present
checklist.description: The output identifies which channels have the largest difference between models (e.g., last-click vs linear), not just raw totals

checklist.name: Last-click assigns full credit to final touch
checklist.description: In results.json, last-click model assigns 100% of each order's revenue to a single channel (the last touchpoint), not split across channels

checklist.name: Linear splits evenly
checklist.description: In results.json, the linear model splits revenue equally across all touchpoints in a path (e.g., a 4-touch path gets 25% each)
